name: agent-project-structure

positive_triggers.input: I'm starting a new agent project, help me set up the structure
positive_triggers.reason: New agent project, needs canonical structure

positive_triggers.input: Our repo has scattered .md files everywhere, organize them
I want to open-source my agent project, what's the standard layout?
positive_triggers.reason: Standard layout needed for public release

negative_triggers.input: Refactor the user service to use the new error format
Build a simple CLI todo app
negative_triggers.reason: Non-agent project, structure template doesn't apply

behavioral_evals.scenario: User asks: 'I'm starting a new agent project'
expected_behavior: Agent identifies project type (RAG / tool-using / production agent / etc.)
Agent initializes ONLY relevant folders (not all 10 by default)
Agent creates README in each folder explaining its purpose
Agent bridges to pwf: task_plan.md / findings.md / progress.md stay at root, NOT inside 01_planner/
Agent applies naming convention (lowercase-hyphenated.md, numeric prefix for stable order)
Agent inventories existing .md files in repo root
Agent classifies each by content (plan / memory / docs / etc.)
Agent moves them into corresponding folders (or merges)
Agent avoids duplicating pwf-managed files (task_plan.md etc.)
Agent updates any references to old paths

notes: Behavioral evals are warning-level until promoted via upstream issue tracker.
